text: In this nearly empty lecture hall, I feel a peculiar anticipation, as if I am early not just for a talk but for an unfolding idea. Discussions of limited editions and databases hint at value and scarcity, pressing on the tension between sharing knowledge and restricting it, between the collective and the exclusive. I am curious, but also distanced, as though the real conversation is yet to begin. Then, moving into the R+D exhibit, the reflection deepens. The wall asks what is interesting, and being here among these questions and experiments, I feel the mundane world is somehow more vivid. I feel inquisitive, a little detached, and oddly comforted by the thought that every moment can become a philosophical adventure if I linger with it long enough.
